description: Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act, 2019 - Central penalty schedule (maximum limits)
notes: These are the maximum penalties under the central act. States may adopt lower amounts.

177.offense: General offenses — violation of any provision of the Act
177.notes: Catch-all for offenses not specified elsewhere

177A.offense: Violation of road regulations made by state government
177A.notes: Contravention of road regulations under state rules

178.offense: Travelling without ticket / refusing to pay fare in public transport
178.notes: Applicable to stage carriage passengers; also covers refusal to ply contract carriage

179.offense: Disobedience of orders of authority / refusal to give information
179.notes: Includes wilfully withholding or giving false information

180.notes: Owner liability for permitting unlicensed person to drive

182.notes: Driving when disqualified by court/authority order

182B.offense: Overloading of passengers in goods vehicle
182B.notes: Rs 2,000 base + Rs 1,000 per extra passenger

183.license_action: Seizure of licence for repeat offence
183.notes: For LMV. Heavy vehicles: Rs 4,000 first / Rs 8,000 repeat

183_heavy.offense: Exceeding speed limit (Medium/Heavy goods or passenger vehicle)
183_heavy.license_action: Seizure of licence for repeat offence
183_heavy.notes: Applicable to medium/heavy goods and passenger motor vehicles

184.imprisonment: Up to 6 months (first), up to 2 years (repeat)
184.notes: Driving in a manner dangerous to public

185.offense: Drunken driving / driving under influence of drugs
185.imprisonment: Up to 6 months (first), up to 2 years (repeat)

186.offense: Driving when mentally or physically unfit

187.offense: Offences relating to accidents — failing to report or fleeing
187.imprisonment: Up to 6 months (simple injury), up to 2 years (grievous hurt/death)
187.notes: Fine up to Rs 5,000 for simple injury; imprisonment + fine for serious cases. Hit-and-run compensation: Rs 2,00,000 (death), Rs 50,000 (grievous hurt)

189.offense: Racing and speed trials without written permission
189.notes: Requires written consent of State Government

190.offense: Using vehicle in unsafe condition / without registration
190.notes: Vehicle that involves danger to public or doesn't comply with requirements

192A.notes: Applicable to transport vehicles; minimum Rs 5,000

193.notes: New section added by 2019 amendment for ride-hailing services

194B.notes: Helmet must conform to BIS standards (IS 4151)

194C.offense: Not giving way to emergency vehicles
194C.notes: Ambulance, fire engine, police vehicle on emergency duty

194D.offense: Using handheld mobile phone while driving

194F.notes: Carrying more than one pillion rider

196.offense: Driving transport vehicle without valid fitness certificate
196.notes: Minimum fine of Rs 5,000 for transport vehicles

199.notes: Guardian deemed guilty; juvenile treated under JJ Act. Vehicle registration cancelled.
